Regenerate neighbor graphs and UMAPs.

Source: R/general.R
regen_reduc.Rd

Regenerates PCA, neighbor graphs, and UMAP for a Seurat object. This is useful if you subset a Seurat object and need to recompute these reductions for the new object. It can also be used to regenerate these reductions if you have modified the object in a way that requires them to be redone (e.g. filtering out cells or genes).

Usage

regen_reduc(
  seurat_obj,
  pca_name = "rpca",
  assay = "RNA",
  num_dims = 20,
  k_param = 20,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

seurat_obj

The Seurat object.

pca_name

Name of the PCA reduction to use for neighbor finding and UMAP. This should be the name of an existing PCA reduction in the Seurat object (e.g. "pca" or "rpca").

assay

Name of the assay to use for neighbor finding and UMAP.

num_dims

Number of PCA dimensions to use for neighbor finding.

k_param

Number of nearest neighbors.

verbose

Logical indicating whether or not to print messages.

Value

A processed Seurat object with the graphs, neighbors, and reductions slots filled in or updated.

Details

If you are providing a reduction based on batch effect integration (e.g. Harmony, RPCA), then you should use the name of that reduction for pca_name. Although this function is called "regen_reduc", it can also be run to generate reductions for the first time (e.g. if you have already run PCA and just want to calculate neighbors and run UMAP).
